hero.headline: Neutral infrastructure for digital identity with holder consent
hero.subheadline: We connect issuers, verifiers, and users without centralizing data. Companies reduce verification costs; users maintain sovereignty over their credentials.

product.title: What we do
product.description: Ultima Forma provides an orchestration layer that connects credential issuers, verifiers, and identity wallets. Users store credentials in sovereign wallets and decide what to share. Companies perform verifications via API, always with explicit and granular consent.

feature1.title: Neutral orchestration
feature1.desc: We connect the ecosystem without centralizing data. The choice remains with issuers, verifiers, and holders.

feature2.title: Sovereign wallet
feature2.desc: Credentials under user control. Decentralized storage and minimum necessary sharing.

feature3.title: Interoperability
feature3.desc: Open standards ensure credentials and flows work beyond a single provider.

pedro.name: Pedro Drummond
pedro.description: Expert in digital identification and identity resolution with Big Data, applied in high-criticality government environments (Federal Police, ABIN, Central Bank of Brazil, Attorney General's Office). Expertise in governance, data quality, deduplication, matching and auditing, with sensitivity to security and privacy constraints.

yuri.name: Yuri Lima
yuri.description: Market-reference expert in building AI-first systems focused on reliability, cost, and security. AI as a pillar from conception: automation, intelligent assistance, evals, observability, and prompt/model governance. Ability to incorporate automation into workflows without compromising compliance requirements in regulated environments.

diagram.verifierHover: Companies and institutions that need to verify user identity attributes

walletStory.title: Daniel didn’t upload a single PDF to finance his new home.
walletStory.subtitle: With one consent click, the bank received authentic, verified, and up-to-date documents.
walletStory.scene1: Buying a home used to mean scanning, uploading, renaming, and resending documents.
walletStory.scene2: Now, his verified identity lives securely in his wallet.
walletStory.scene3: He sees exactly what will be shared. He taps Authorize.
walletStory.scene4: The bank receives structured, cryptographically signed credentials — not PDFs.
walletStory.consentMessage: Bank requests verified identity and income credentials.
walletStory.final: No uploads. No fraud. No delays.
walletStory.cred4: No centralized storage of personal data

providerStory.title: Atlas Bank no longer spends time validating documents.
providerStory.subtitle: It receives verified, cryptographically signed identity credentials.
providerStory.scene1: Every credit request came with uploads. PDFs. Images. Editable files. Manual review. Operational cost. Fraud exposure.
providerStory.scene2: Atlas Bank stopped asking for files. It started requesting verified credentials.
providerStory.scene3: The customer sees exactly what will be shared. One tap. Explicit consent.
providerStory.scene4: Atlas Bank receives structured, signed credentials. Authenticity is cryptographically provable. No manual file validation required.
providerStory.consentMessage: Atlas Bank requests verified identity and income credentials.
providerStory.final:
Lower cost.
Lower fraud.
Faster credit decisions.
providerStory.cred4: No centralized storage of personal data

verifierStory.title: The State no longer issues static documents.
verifierStory.subtitle: It issues verifiable digital trust.
verifierStory.scene1: For decades, governments issued documents. Once issued, they were copied, stored, and replicated beyond control.
verifierStory.scene2: Now, identity is digitally signed at the source.
verifierStory.scene3: Institutional partners verify authenticity directly. No file uploads. No uncontrolled copies.
verifierStory.scene4: The State governs the lifecycle of digital identity. Issuance. Verification. Revocation.
verifierStory.final:
Public infrastructure.
Private interoperability.
Systemic trust.

hero.headline: Your data passes through dozens of hands. None of them are yours.
hero.sub: Every company collects your documents from scratch. Every verification costs money, time, and trust. We built the infrastructure that changes this.

problem.headline: Identity verification is broken at every level.
problem.sub: Users repeat the same process dozens of times. Companies spend fortunes verifying what others already verified. Data gets outdated before it's even used.
problem.before2: Same user submits documents to every new institution
problem.before4: Data becomes outdated without automatic sync
problem.before5: Centralized databases are high-value attack targets
problem.after1: From R$ 3.90 per verification via API
problem.after2: Credential issued once, reusable across contexts
problem.after3: Onboarding in seconds with verified credentials
problem.after4: Attributes updated at the source with consent
problem.after5: No centralized credential storage — reduced attack surface

solution.headline: Neutral infrastructure for verifiable credentials.
solution.sub: Ultima Forma orchestrates the connection between credential issuers, verifiers, and users — without centralizing data. The holder controls what to share, when, and with whom.
solution.positioning: Ultima Forma is the neutral infrastructure that orchestrates identity credentials with holder consent, enabling companies to reduce KYC costs and users to maintain sovereignty over their data.
solution.card1Desc: User-controlled app with sovereign storage. Granular consent per verification. W3C Verifiable Credentials standard.
solution.card2Desc: Backend connecting issuers, verifiers, and wallets. Manages flows, DID resolution, signature validation, and audit logs.
solution.card3Desc: Integration interface for verifiers. Request specific credentials, receive attested results, integrate with existing KYC/AML flows.
solution.notBankDesc: No deposits, credit, or financial products
solution.notCreditDesc: No credit risk assessment or lending

steps.headline: From request to verification in 6 steps.
steps.sub: A transparent flow where the user always controls what is shared.
steps.step1Desc: A company needs to verify specific attributes (e.g., Individual Taxpayer Registry, address, income) via our application programming interface.
steps.step2Desc: Our platform forwards the request to the user's wallet with the policy and scope.
steps.step3Desc: The holder sees exactly which attributes will be shared and with whom. They choose to authorize or deny.
steps.step4Desc: The wallet sends a verifiable presentation with the selected attributes and cryptographic evidence.
steps.step5Desc: Orchestration validates the issuer's signature, checks revocation status when applicable, and records event metadata.
steps.step6Desc: The verifier receives the attested result and continues their process. An immutable audit trail is maintained.

diagram.title: Operations between issuers, consent, and verifiers
diagram.verifierDetail: The verifier (fintech, bank, etc.) requests verification of specific attributes via application programming interface. A company needs to validate Individual Taxpayer Registry, address, income or other user data for know your customer processes, onboarding or compliance.
diagram.walletDetail: The user's wallet stores credentials issued by authorities. The holder sees exactly which attributes will be shared and with whom, and chooses to authorize or deny. The wallet sends a verifiable presentation with selected attributes.
diagram.orchestrationDetail: Our platform forwards the request to the user's wallet with policy and scope. It validates the issuer's signature, checks revocation status when applicable, and records event metadata. Delivers the attested result to the verifier.
diagram.issuersDetail: Issuers (Gov.br, Federal Revenue, institutions) issue verifiable credentials signed cryptographically. These credentials are stored in the holder's wallet, who controls what to share.
diagram.arrowRequestDetail: Request via application programming interface with required attributes
diagram.storyPhase0Long: Authorities (Gov.br, Federal Revenue, institutions) issue cryptographically signed credentials. Users collect them in their wallets.
diagram.storyPhase1Long: The verifier receives the credential as proof. This establishes trust in the data source before the verification flow starts.
diagram.storyPhase2Long: The company requests verification via API. Ultima Forma receives the request and routes it to the user's wallet with policy and scope.
diagram.storyPhase3Long: The wallet displays exactly which attributes are requested and by whom. The holder sees the verification policy before consenting.
diagram.storyPhase4: User consents; verifiable presentation is sent
diagram.storyPhase4Long: The user authorizes the share. The wallet creates a verifiable presentation with only the selected attributes and sends it back through Ultima Forma.
diagram.storyPhase5: Attested result returns to the verifier
diagram.storyPhase5Long: Ultima Forma validates signatures, checks revocation, and records metadata. The verifier receives an attested result and a permanent audit trail.
diagram.playHint: See the flow step by step
diagram.storyClosing: This flow uses W3C verifiable credentials, cryptographic signatures, and granular consent policies. Ultima Forma orchestrates communication between issuers, wallets, and verifiers, ensuring an immutable audit trail and LGPD compliance.

consent.title: Consent Simulator
consent.name: Full Name
consent.footer: Only selected attributes are shared. You control what goes out.

personas.headline: One infrastructure, multiple perspectives.
personas.userDesc: No more scanning, uploading, and re-sending documents to every institution. Your verified credentials live in your wallet. You decide what to share, with whom, and when. One tap replaces 15 minutes of forms.
personas.bankTitle: The Bank / FinTech: Cost and Compliance
personas.bankDesc: Replace R$ 40–100 per KYC verification with R$ 3.90 per API call. Receive cryptographically signed credentials instead of PDFs. Reduce manual review, fraud exposure, and onboarding friction. Compliance audit trails included.
personas.insuranceDesc: Receive automatic, consent-based updates when a policyholder's verified attributes change — address, income, marital status. No more outdated records, no periodic re-registration campaigns. The first use case: automatic data sync between a FinTech and an Insurer.
personas.govDesc: Issue verifiable digital credentials instead of static documents. Control the lifecycle: issuance, verification, revocation. Enable institutional partners to verify authenticity directly, without uncontrolled copies.

arch.headline: Pragmatic today. Decentralized tomorrow.
arch.sub: Our architecture supports a pragmatic MVP with cloud-backed wallet and a fully device-based model. Toggle to compare.
arch.prag1Desc: User credentials stored encrypted in cloud with device-key access. Enables cross-device experience and backup.
arch.prag2Desc: Lower integration complexity for issuers and verifiers. Standard REST APIs. Minimal client-side requirements.
arch.prag3Desc: SLA guarantees, audit trails, managed infrastructure. Meets enterprise compliance and security requirements.
arch.dev1Desc: Credentials stored exclusively on user's device. True sovereign control. No cloud dependency.
arch.dev2Desc: Selective disclosure and zero-knowledge proofs. Reveal only necessary attributes without exposing full credentials.
arch.dev3Desc: Full alignment with W3C Verifiable Credentials and DID specifications. Maximum interoperability.

market.headline: A $15–25B market waiting for neutral infrastructure.
market.moatSummary: Each new participant increases value for all others. Trust infrastructure is cumulative and hard to replicate. Integration depth creates switching cost. Neutrality and open standards prevent lock-in.

biz.headline: Three revenue streams. High margins. Built for recurrence.
biz.stream1Desc: Pay per API call. Basic (R$ 3.90) or Qualified (R$ 12.90) with volume discounts.
biz.stream2Desc: Monthly/annual plans with included volume and cost predictability.
biz.stream3Desc: Annual contracts with 99.9% uptime, dedicated support, and assisted integration.

roadmap.headline: 36 months. Four phases. Clear milestones.
roadmap.phase0Desc: Functional MVP, Enterprise API, integration with first credential issuer, minimal wallet (Android/iOS).
roadmap.phase1Desc: 1 issuer in production, validated end-to-end flow, 1 anchor partner, ~10k verifications.
roadmap.phase2Desc: 2–3 integrated issuers, 5–10 enterprise clients, 200–500k verifications/year, first subscription and SLA contracts.
roadmap.phase3Desc: Multi-region preparation, vertical expansion, 1–2M verifications/year, Series A preparation.

risks.headline: We see the risks. Here's how we mitigate them.
risks.sub: Transparent risk assessment with concrete mitigation strategies.
risks.regulatoryImpact: Regulatory change could reclassify Ultima Forma (e.g., as financial institution or expanded data controller).
risks.regulatoryMitigation: Prior legal opinion, conservative architecture (no credential storage, no credit decisions), monitoring of regulations, participation in regulatory sandboxes.
risks.adoptionImpact: Market may not adopt sovereign identity or orchestration at expected pace.
risks.adoptionMitigation: Focus on use cases with clear ROI, pilot with anchor partner, messaging oriented to business value, flexibility for hybrid flows.
risks.bigtechImpact: Large platforms could launch competing offerings with superior distribution.
risks.bigtechMitigation: Focus on regulated verticals where Big Tech faces restrictions, emphasis on neutrality and interoperability, building the network before potential entry.
risks.techImpact: Security vulnerability, infrastructure failure, or standards obsolescence.
risks.techMitigation: Mature standards (W3C VC, DID), security audits, infrastructure redundancy, minimal attack surface architecture.
risks.executionImpact: Failure to deliver on time, close clients, or retain talent.
risks.executionMitigation: Realistic roadmap with incremental milestones, critical hiring, adequate runway, continuous metric tracking.

ask.headline: We're raising R$ 3.5M to build the future of identity.
ask.ms1: MVP in production with basic and qualified verification
ask.ms3: MRR R$ 40–80k at end of period
ask.ms4: At least 1 replicable use case with playbook

team.headline: Built by experts in identity, AI, and regulated environments.
team.pedroDesc: Expert in digital identification and identity resolution with Big Data. High-criticality government environments: Federal Police, ABIN, Central Bank of Brazil, Attorney General's Office.
team.yuriDesc: Market-reference expert in AI-first systems. Reliability, cost, and security. Automation and governance in regulated environments.

trust.headline: Trust Center
trust.sub: How Ultima Forma protects data, ensures compliance, and maintains transparency.
trust.minimizationDesc: We collect only what's necessary to operate orchestration: consent logs and transaction metadata. Never credential content.
trust.consentDesc: Every verification requires explicit, granular, and revocable consent from the holder. Evidence is recorded.
trust.noStorageDesc: We do not store credentials or user identity data. Sovereign storage remains with the holder's wallet.
trust.cryptoDesc: Credentials signed by issuers with verifiable keys. DIDs for endpoint resolution. W3C Verifiable Credentials standard.
trust.lgpdApproach: Data minimization, explicit consent, holder rights (access, rectification, deletion, portability). We act as processor/facilitator.
trust.gdprApproach: Same principles as LGPD with international transfer mechanisms (standard clauses, adequacy decisions).
trust.eidasApproach: Architecture aligned with digital identity wallet requirements. Ready for qualification as trust service provider.
trust.amlApproach: We orchestrate, not decide. AML/KYC due diligence responsibility remains with verifiers. Consent logs enable traceable audit.
trust.issuersDesc: Responsible for quality and validity of issued credentials.
trust.verifiersDesc: Responsible for decisions based on credentials (KYC, credit, etc.).
trust.ufDesc: Responsible for orchestration operation, availability, and processing compliance. Not responsible for credential content or verifier decisions.
trust.deepDiveHint: Want the full regulatory and security analysis?
